sections.stepTitle: React to everything with full energy!
sections.meaning: This is absolutely amazing! / No way!

scenes.text: You try Korean BBQ for the first time. This is the only acceptable response.
You see the Seoul skyline from Namsan Tower at night. Say it out loud!
Your Korean friend does something impressive. React naturally!

sections.stepTitle: Korean slang you've been hearing in K-dramas!

korean.reading: jin-jja dae-ba-gi-e-yo! — really + daebak + it is!

sections.insight: 진짜 (jin-jja) = really / truly / seriously. 대박 (dae-bak) started as slang but is now everyday Korean — it means amazing, jackpot, no way! Add 이에요 to make it a sentence. You can also just shout '대박!' on its own.

sections.instruction: "진짜 ___" = "Really ___" or "This is really ___". Try each one! 👇

questions.question: You just tried Korean BBQ and it's incredible. What do you say?
questions.correctMsg: Yes!! 진짜 대박이에요! is the perfect response. Koreans will love your enthusiasm!
questions.wrongMsg: Not quite! 진짜 대박이에요! is your go-to reaction to anything amazing — food, views, surprises!

questions.question: What does 진짜 mean?
questions.sub: Think about how you've heard it in K-dramas!
questions.correctMsg: Correct! 진짜 = really / seriously / for real. It's one of the most useful intensifiers in Korean!
questions.wrongMsg: Not quite! 진짜 (jin-jja) means 'really / seriously / for real' — you've definitely heard it in K-dramas!

culture.title: You're ready for Seoul!
culture.body:
You now know how to:
✅ Get attention: 저기요!
✅ Order food and drinks: ___ 주세요
✅ Check into your hotel: 체크인 하려고요
✅ Navigate the subway: 어디서 갈아타요?
✅ Shop at markets: 얼마예요?
✅ React like a local: 진짜 대박이에요!

Most importantly — Koreans absolutely love when foreigners try Korean. Even one word will make someone smile. You've got this. 화이팅! (hwa-i-ting!) — Go for it!

drama.japanese: "Fighting! / You can do it!" — a Korean cheer
drama.note: 화이팅 comes from the English word 'fighting' — it's a cheer of encouragement. You'll hear it everywhere ✨
